description: Literary fiction requires a central consciousness whose interiority is the primary narrative engine, mirror characters who apply pressure on inner life, and objects that accrue meaning through repetition — the ending must be resonant without being vague.

character_constraints.description: A character whose self-deceptions, contradictions, and inner life are the primary subject — interiority is the engine.
A character who applies pressure on the protagonist's interiority — exposing what they hide from themselves.

relationship_constraints.description: Other characters exist to expose the protagonist's inner life — relationships serve interior revelation, not external plot.

place_constraints.description: A mundane location that accumulates thematic weight through the narrative — the house, the garden, the office becomes a symbol.

object_constraints.description: An object that accrues meaning through repetition and transformation — its significance deepens with each appearance.
An object functioning on both literal and symbolic levels simultaneously.

element_rules.description: Character interiority must be the primary narrative engine — plot serves interior revelation, not the reverse.
element_rules.testable_condition: At least 3 major timeline transitions are initiated by the protagonist's internal realization or self-confrontation.

element_rules.description: The ending must be resonant without being vague — earned ambiguity is required, trailing off is forbidden.
element_rules.testable_condition: The final scene resolves or reframes the central interior question, even if it does not resolve the external situation.
